Equipment selection menu visible (Atraumatic Grasper, Bipolar KELLY Grasper, Scissors, Attraumatic Grasper); indicating procedural progression
Uterus centered in field with visible coagulated fallopian tubes bilaterally (pale/white areas on both isthmic segments); instruction indicates documenting complete coagulation of isthmus

confidence_rationale: This video is educational content demonstrating TUBAL STERILIZATION via BIPOLAR COAGULATION, NOT the FLS Task 3 Endoloop procedure. The rubric for task3_endoloop requires: (1) pre-tied ligating loop deployment around a foam appendage, (2) loop positioning and cinching, and (3) tail cut inside trainer. This video shows: bilateral fallopian tube coagulation using bipolar electrosurgery on a realistic reproductive anatomy model. No endoloop is used. No foam organ is present. No loop placement, advancement, or tail cutting occurs. The procedures are completely distinct from an FLS perspective.
cannot_determine: FLS Task 3 Endoloop score — this is not an endoloop procedure
Completion time for task3_endoloop — procedure shown is tubal sterilization by coagulation
Loop placement accuracy — no loop is used in this procedure
Loop security or cinching quality — no loop present
technique_summary: This video is INSTRUCTIONAL CONTENT demonstrating bilateral tubal sterilization by bipolar electrosurgery coagulation. It is NOT demonstrating the FLS Task 3 Endoloop. Scoring against the task3_endoloop rubric is inappropriate and impossible.
strengths: Clear procedural demonstration of bilateral tubal sterilization technique
Good tissue visualization and manipulation using uterine manipulator
Proper bipolar grasper application to marked anatomical targets
Educational guidance overlays showing step progression

demonstrated_techniques: Bilateral tubal sterilization via bipolar electrosurgery coagulation
Coagulation of first 3 cm of each fallopian isthmus using bipolar KELLY grasper
Uterine manipulator use for tissue retraction and visualization
Identification and marking of target segments (fallopian tube isthmus)
Proper grasper positioning and activation for hemostatic coagulation
Procedural documentation by capturing bilateral coagulated tubes in final image
verbal_cues: Coagulate the first 3 cm of the left isthmus using a bipolar KELLY grasper. Cut in the coagulated area using scissors (optional).
Using the 'Uterine manipulator' assistant, move the uterus ventrally and to the left to put tension on the right adnexa.
Coagulate the first 3 cm of the right isthmus using a bipolar KELLY grasper. Cut in the coagulated area using scissors (optional).
Using the 'Uterine manipulator' assistant, move the uterus ventrally and capture an image of both fallopian tubes to document complete coagulation of the isthmus.
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding under-coagulation by ensuring adequate tissue blanching and color change
Proper tissue stabilization and retraction using the uterine manipulator
Correct identification of target anatomical landmarks (isthmus vs. ampulla)
Bilateral completion to ensure adequate sterilization

critical_classification_note: **TASK MISMATCH DETECTED**: This video demonstrates TUBAL STERILIZATION BY BIPOLAR COAGULATION, a gynecologic surgical procedure. It does NOT demonstrate FLS Task 3 (Endoloop - ligating loop deployment on a foam organ appendage). The two procedures are entirely different. NO FLS TASK 3 SCORE CAN BE GENERATED FROM THIS VIDEO.
